Here is a pytest-style test suite in plain text (no Markdown code blocks). You can place this into a test file such as test_camel_to_snake.py.

from your_module import camel_to_snake
import pytest

def test_happy_path_simple():
    result = camel_to_snake("CamelCase")
    assert result == "camel_case"

def test_happy_path_multiple_transitions():
    result = camel_to_snake("ThisIsATestString")
    assert result == "this_is_a_test_string"

def test_happy_path_single_word_lower():
    result = camel_to_snake("word")
    assert result == "word"

def test_happy_path_single_word_upper():
    result = camel_to_snake("WORD")
    assert result == "word"

def test_edge_empty_string():
    result = camel_to_snake("")
    assert result == ""

def test_edge_already_snake():
    result = camel_to_snake("already_snake")
    assert result == "already_snake"

def test_edge_mixed_characters():
    result = camel_to_snake("JSONData2024File")
    # Only breaks lower->upper transitions; numbers unaffected
    assert result == "jsondata2024_file"

def test_edge_leading_uppercase_sequence():
    result = camel_to_snake("XMLHttpRequest")
    # First lowercase->uppercase boundary is before H
    assert result == "xml_http_request"

def test_error_non_string_input():
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        camel_to_snake(None)

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        camel_to_snake(123)

    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        camel_to_snake(["Not", "A", "String"])
